string.text: The StG 44 (short for Sturmgewehr 44, "assault rifle 44") is a German selective-fire assault rifle developed during World War II by Hugo Schmeisser. It was also known under its early designations MP 43 and MP 44 (Maschinenpistole 43 and 44). The StG 44 was the first successful assault rifle, featuring an intermediate cartridge, controllable automatic fire, a more compact design compared to a battle rifle with higher rate of fire, and was primarily intended to engage targets at distances of up to several hundred meters.
